title: SwiftData Enhances Queries, Adds Support for External Types and Data Store Observation
gist: Apple's 2027 SwiftData release adds Codable-based support for persisting third-party types, SwiftUI list sectioning via a new @Query parameter, and two new observation APIs (ResultsObserver, HistoryObserver) that extend reactive data-store monitoring outside SwiftUI, continuing SwiftData's gradual feature convergence with Core Data.

insights.claim: SwiftData 2027 adds @Attribute(.codable) support letting developers persist third-party or SDK types they don't control (which can't be marked @Model) by conforming them to Codable, removing a longstanding limitation that blocked including such types directly in a model's schema.
insights.speaker: Sergio De Simone
insights.so_what: Closes a specific integration gap that previously forced iOS/macOS developers into workarounds when mixing third-party libraries with SwiftData-backed models.
insights.locator: Codable attributes solve a limitation in SwiftData

insights.claim: Codable-backed properties in SwiftData cannot be used for filtering or sorting (they are treated as opaque values) and do not trigger automatic migrations when the underlying type changes, so Apple's guidance is to avoid using .codable for types the developer actually controls and use @Model instead.
insights.speaker: Sergio De Simone
insights.so_what: A concrete caveat developers need to weigh before adopting the new Codable feature -- it's an escape hatch for external types, not a general-purpose modeling shortcut.
insights.locator: Using a Codable property in a SwiftData model comes with a few limitations

insights.claim: SwiftData 2027 introduces ResultsObserver, offering @Query-like reactivity (sorting, filtering, sectioning) outside SwiftUI contexts such as plain object classes or SceneKit-based apps, and HistoryObserver, which exposes an observable event counter that increments on new persistent-history transactions to keep a data store in sync with remote systems.
insights.speaker: Sergio De Simone
insights.so_what: Extends SwiftData's usability to non-SwiftUI apps and remote-sync use cases, broadening its addressable use cases relative to Core Data.
